Title: Gibberellin dynamics governing nodulation revealed using GIBBERELLIN PERCEPTION SENSOR 2 in Medicago truncatula lateral organs

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sinorhizobium meliloti inoculation during nodulation; paclobutrazol treatment; precursor feeding with GA12 or GA9
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; last biosynthetic step and functionally limiting contributor to nodule GA accumulation, but not solely responsible due to compensation by MtGA3ox2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: nodule primordium cortical cells and mature nodule apex/meristem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes nodule growth, maturation, and nitrogen-fixing organ development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtGA3ox1 is highly expressed during rhizobial symbiosis and was tested genetically using ga3ox1 insertion alleles. 2. ga3ox1 mutants retained some GA signal in nodules, implying partial redundancy, but showed reduced nodule size. 3. Co-expression of MtGA3ox1 with MtGA20ox1 under a NIN promoter increased local GA and enlarged mature nodules.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes local GA accumulation and increases nodule size and proper development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broad promoter expression did not explain the tight spatial GA pattern; likely additional post-transcriptional regulation or catabolism. MtGA3ox2 is suggested as a compensatory paralog.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: final activation step to bioactive GA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering larger nodules by boosting local GA biosynthesis, especially in combination with MtGA20ox1 and nodule-specific promoters.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: bioactive GA (e.g. GA4/GA1 depending pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Synthetic MtNIN promoter for overexpression; native 3 kb promoter analyzed as broadly expressed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function causes dwarfism, slow germination, and short internodes; elevated GA may have pleiotropic growth effects.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during rhizobial symbiosis; broadly expressed in roots; induced during nodulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the last biosynthetic activation step converting inactive GA precursors such as GA9 into bioactive gibberellin.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad root expression during infection; functional effect seen in developing nodules and mature nodule apex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Insertion mutant analysis; promoter-reporter analysis; nlsGPS2 hormone imaging; nodule phenotyping; combinatorial overexpression with MtGA20ox1

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sinorhizobium meliloti inoculation during nodulation; precursor feeding with GA12; NIN-promoter-driven overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Traditionally considered rate-limiting/penultimate biosynthetic enzyme; contributes quantitatively to GA levels but does not solely determine spatial pattern
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: nodule primordium cortical cells and mature nodule apex/meristem
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtGA20ox1 promoter is broadly expressed in roots during nodulation, indicating transcription alone does not explain GA localization. 2. Precursor feeding and co-overexpression with MtGA3ox1 increased local GA. 3. Nodule-specific elevation of MtGA20ox1 together with MtGA3ox1 enlarged mature nodules.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies precursor flux to increase local GA biosynthesis and contributes to larger mature nodules when co-expressed with MtGA3ox1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recent ChIP data cited in discussion suggest LSH1 targets include MtGA20ox1, linking organ identity to GA pathway regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: penultimate step of GA activation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic engineering target for nodule enlargement when stacked with MtGA3ox1 under a nodule-specific promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Synthetic MtNIN promoter for overexpression; native 3 kb promoter analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic GA-growth effects if ectopically expressed broadly.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed in root during nodulation; among highly expressed GA20ox genes during nodulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation steps converting C20-GA precursors such as GA12 toward C19 intermediates including GA9 before final activation by GA3ox.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-reporter analysis; precursor feeding logic; nodule-specific co-overexpression with MtGA3ox1; nlsGPS2 imaging

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sinorhizobium meliloti inoculation during nodulation; tissue-specific misexpression in cortex, epidermis, or NIN domain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key local depletion enzyme used to suppress GA accumulation in specific tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: nodule primordium cortical cells and mature nodule apex/meristem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nodule initiation, growth, maturation, and nitrogen fixation when maintained locally
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Misexpression of MtGAOL1 under a functional NIN promoter reduced total nodule number and eliminated pink nitrogen-fixing nodules. 2. Cortex-specific MtGAOL1 strongly reduced developing nodules, indicating cortical GA is required. 3. Epidermis-specific MtGAOL1 had weaker, nonsignificant effects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Local GA depletion suppresses nodule initiation, growth, and maturation, especially when depletion occurs in cortical cells.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified here as pathway/catabolic enzyme because it enzymatically depletes the focal metabolite. The paper describes it as belonging to the Elongated Uppermost Internode-like cytochrome P450 family.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Elongated Uppermost Internode-like cytochrome P450 monooxygenase family
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tissue-specific suppression of nodule growth; cautions due to strong reduction in nodulation and transformation defects, rescued experimentally with GA3.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with and depletes target product
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Synthetic MtNIN promoter; AtCO2 cortex-specific promoter; MtFLOT4 epidermis-specific promoter
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduces nodule number and abolishes pink nodules; causes transformation defects unless GA3 supplemented.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative deactivation/breakdown of bioactive gibberellin, lowering active GA levels.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tissue-specific misexpression; nodulation phenotyping; comparison of nodule number and pink nodules

Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function abolishes GA accumulation at inoculation sites and blocks proper nodulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for upregulation of MtGA3ox1 and GA accumulation during nodule organogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of the GA program in nodulation; spontaneous nodules can be induced by constitutively active CCaMK but NIN is the tested native regulator for GA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Symbiotic nodule identity regulator during nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GA accumulation at nodule initiation sites and thereby supports proper nodule organogenesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes nodule growth, maturation, and nitrogen-fixing organ development via GA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nin-4 mutant analysis with nlsGPS2 imaging
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream inducer of local GA accumulation during nodule organogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: nodule primordium cortical cells and mature nodule apex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: At nodule initiation sites during symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA fails to accumulate at the site of inoculation in nin-4 mutants. 2. This supports previous evidence that NIN-dependent transcription induces MtGA3ox1. 3. NIN is therefore upstream of the GA program required for nodule organogenesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strategic upstream regulator for coordinating nodule identity and GA-dependent growth, but manipulation must preserve early infection functions.

Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes loss of GA patterning and partial conversion of nodules toward lateral root identity.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Maintains GA biosynthesis/patterning in mature nodules; cited ChIP data include MtGA20ox1 as a target of LSH1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper analyzes lsh1 lsh2 double mutants together; individual gene-specific effects are not separated.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Nodule identity regulators active during nodule maturation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains high GA in the nodule apex and proper nodule identity, size, and maturation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nodule size and mature nodule identity through GA patterning
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: lsh1 lsh2 mutant imaging with nlsGPS2; GA rescue treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller maintaining GA gradient in mature nodules
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional control of organ-identity-linked GA patterning
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sinorhizobium meliloti inoculation; post-initiation GA4 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA accumulation patterning and maintenance during nodule maturation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lsh1 lsh2 nodules show reduced GA in the apex and increased GA in surrounding tissue. 2. Converted root-like organs have lower overall GA. 3. Post-initiation GA treatment rescues nodule size, linking LSH-dependent identity to GA control.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for maintaining nodule identity and size, but GA supplementation cannot fully rescue organ identity defects.

Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severe organ conversion toward lateral root-like structures with reduced GA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Maintains GA gradient indirectly through nodule identity regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper analyzes noot1 noot2 double mutants together; individual gene-specific effects are not separated.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to sustain nodule-specific GA accumulation and prevent conversion to lateral root-like identity.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports mature nodule identity and GA-associated growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: noot1 noot2 mutant imaging with nlsGPS2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking organ identity maintenance to GA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sinorhizobium meliloti inoculation; post-initiation GA4 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA patterning maintenance during nodule maturation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. noot1 noot2 mutants have markedly reduced GA in nodule apices. 2. Organs converted toward lateral root-like structures show lower overall GA. 3. GA loss correlates with loss of nodule identity but GA treatment does not rescue organ conversion.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for preserving nodule identity; not sufficient to manipulate GA alone without broader identity network control.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GA patterning program; specific direct targets not established in this paper
